meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass: `make` runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. NEVER commit with failing checks. Pipe long output through `tail -200` to avoid context window exhaustion.
Rule of Two is MANDATORY before every commit: two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews of the exact same diff. Context-contamination resets to 0. Use strict-review-gate skill protocol EXACTLY.
Each task follows: work → check (make) → Rule of Two (strict-review-gate) → Commit (git-commit skill). Cycles are mandatory on failure until task is ACTUALLY COMPLETE AND CORRECT.

JS runs in Goja event loop — ALL blocking operations freeze the BubbleTea UI. Never call synchronous exec.execv() in update() or in async Promise continuations that run on the Goja microtask queue. Use exec.spawn() + await for all subprocess calls, or tea.tick() to yield between steps.
super_document_script.js is the REFERENCE implementation for viewport/scrollbar/mouse/keyboard patterns. ALL TUI code must follow its patterns exactly.
internal/termmux must remain clean of pr-split-specific logic. It will be extracted to its own module. Only general-purpose terminal multiplexer functionality belongs there.

The Mux VTerm captures ALL child output via teeLoop. Mux.ChildExitOutput() → vtm.String() (plain text). Mux.ChildScreen() → vtm.RenderFullScreen() (ANSI). CaptureSession has both output() and screen() (ANSI). Always use ANSI methods for visual rendering and plain text methods for assertions/diagnostics.

exec.spawn() returns a Promise-based ChildHandle with stdout/stderr ReadableStreams and await-able wait(). This is the REQUIRED interface for non-blocking subprocess operations in async JS contexts. exec.execv() is ONLY acceptable in REPL mode or pre-BubbleTea contexts.

tuiMux is the global Mux instance connecting the TUI to the Claude child process. It is created Go-side (pr_split.go) and injected as a JS global. pollClaudeScreenshot() reads tuiMux.childScreen() for ANSI. writeToChild() sends keyboard input. These MUST be connected end-to-end for the Claude pane to function.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are sequential. Complete one fully (including Rule of Two + commit) before starting the next. Each task is self-contained. Break work into manageable units.
mandatoryDirectives.claudeIntegrationMandate: The core value proposition of osm pr-split is DEEP Claude integration with an interactive, muxed terminal pane embedded in the TUI. Every wizard phase must surface Claude's capabilities: prompting, classification, plan review feedback, error diagnosis, conflict resolution. The Claude pane must be ANSI-rendered, accept keyboard input when focused, and be switchable between screenshot mode and full passthrough.
mandatoryDirectives.eventLoopMandate: The Goja event loop processes microtasks (Promise continuations) after each _updateFn call. ANY blocking call in a Promise continuation freezes the UI until the call returns. All subprocess calls in async contexts MUST use exec.spawn() + await. Heuristic tick handlers MUST yield between operations via tea.tick().
mandatoryDirectives.integrationTestMandate: Real-Claude integration tests MUST validate all Claude interaction flows end-to-end. Run via `make integration-test-prsplit`. These tests spawn real Claude processes, exercise muxed terminal pane rendering, verify ANSI output, and test keyboard input forwarding. They are the ultimate proof the system works.

statusSection.goalState: A fully functional, production-quality pr-split TUI wizard with: (1) ANSI-rendered interactive Claude terminal pane with keyboard forwarding WORKING END-TO-END, (2) non-blocking async operations via exec.spawn throughout ALL TUI paths (heuristic AND auto), (3) proper viewport/scrollbar on all screens, (4) working keyboard+mouse navigation matching super-document patterns including in split-view mode, (5) real Claude integration with back-and-forth interaction in EVERY wizard phase, (6) embedded terminal output as BubbleTea component via termmux Mux with live ANSI rendering, (7) proper signal handling, (8) working error resolution flow with Claude diagnostics, (9) post-split verification with live CaptureSession output, (10) correct colors/layout with WCAG AA compliance, (11) comprehensive test coverage including real-Claude integration tests and VTerm observation tests, (12) exhaustive documentation of flows, architecture, and interactive patterns, (13) process output visible during all long-running operations via muxed CaptureSession panes.

sequentialTasks.task: T32: Run integration-test-prsplit target and document failure baseline
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make integration-test-prsplit` with real Claude to establish a truthful baseline of what works and what is broken. This MUST come first — you cannot fix what you haven't measured. Steps: (a) Run `make integration-test-prsplit` and capture full output. (b) Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` for mock tests. (c) Document each test: name, pass/fail, failure reason, blocking call if any. (d) If tests hang (expected due to blocking event loop), use timeout + kill to capture state. (e) Add any discovered failures as new tasks to the blueprint immediately. (f) Save baseline report to scratch/integration-test-baseline.md. (g) Run the full test suite `make make-all-with-log` and confirm non-integration tests pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Baseline report exists at scratch/integration-test-baseline.md documenting every integration test name, pass/fail status, and failure reason
Full `make` (non-integration tests) passes with zero failures
Any newly discovered issues are added to blueprint.json as new tasks
Report includes specific failure analysis per test, including blocking call identification for hung tests

sequentialTasks.task: T33: Fix tuiMux bootstrap — ensure Claude process attaches to Mux in TUI context
sequentialTasks.description: The tuiMux global exists in JS (pr_split_16_tui_core.js:4) and has childScreen()/writeToChild() calls (lines 2783, 451), but the bootstrap path from Go → JS is incomplete: Claude is spawned by automatedSplit() pipeline (pr_split_10_pipeline.js) but NOT attached to the Mux that the TUI views read from. Steps: (a) In pr_split.go, verify the Mux instance created Go-side is injected as the `tuiMux` JS global. Trace the exact injection path. (b) In pr_split_10_pipeline.js automatedSplit(), after Claude executor.spawn(), call tuiMux.attach(handle) where handle is the Claude ChildHandle. (c) Verify the attachment survives across wizard states (Mux not garbage collected). (d) In pr_split_13_tui.js, verify the TUI init function properly sets up tuiMux references BEFORE pollClaudeScreenshot starts. (e) Add a guard in pollClaudeScreenshot (line 2773): if tuiMux is undefined or has no child, show 'No Claude attached' instead of silently failing. (f) Test: start wizard with auto strategy, verify childScreen() returns non-empty string after Claude spawns.
acceptanceCriteria: tuiMux.childScreen() returns non-empty ANSI output from Claude after executor.spawn() completes
tuiMux.writeToChild() sends input to Claude PTY and input is received by the child process
Split-view (Ctrl+L) shows live Claude output rendered from childScreen()
pollClaudeScreenshot guard shows 'No Claude attached' when tuiMux is undefined or has no child

context.deliverables: Architecture verified connected end-to-end. pollClaudeScreenshot improved with hasChild() guard. switchTo guarded at all 3 call sites with backward-compatible pattern. 6 new unit tests all PASS.

sequentialTasks.task: T34: Convert runAnalysisStep to async Promise+poll pattern
sequentialTasks.description: runAnalysisStep() at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:1938 is called from tick handlers and directly calls synchronous functions that block the event loop: analyzeDiff() at line 1951, applyStrategy() at line 1983, createSplitPlan() at line 1997, validatePlan() at line 2015. Async versions of these functions EXIST (created in T31) but are NOT called. Convert to Promise+poll: (a) Create startAnalysisAsync(s) that launches each step as a Promise using the *Async variants: analyzeDiffAsync(), applyStrategyAsync() (if exists, else wrap), createSplitPlanAsync(). Store Promise in s.analysisPending. Start polling via tea.tick(100, 'analysis-poll'). (b) Create handleAnalysisPoll(s) that checks if s.analysisPending has resolved: if no → continue polling; if yes → extract result, advance step, chain next async operation. (c) Update _updateFn tick dispatch: replace analysis-step-N ticks with analysis-poll. (d) Remove synchronous runAnalysisStep function. (e) Verify: during heuristic analysis, the UI remains responsive — send mouse/key events during analysis and confirm they process. Spinner updates between steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Heuristic analysis path is fully non-blocking — UI remains responsive during all analysis steps
Progress spinner animates during analysis (frames cycle at ~100ms)
Cancel via Ctrl+C processes within 1 second during any analysis step
View renders correctly between analysis steps with proper state transitions
All analysis-related tests pass
Zero remaining calls to sync analyzeDiff/applyStrategy/createSplitPlan from TUI tick handlers (verified by grep)
sequentialTasks.deliverables: Replaced sync runAnalysisStep with async runAnalysisAsync + handleAnalysisPoll. analyzeDiffAsync and createSplitPlanAsync used instead of sync versions. 100ms poll tick for responsive spinner. Cancel checks between all steps. 9 new tests (4 poll-state + 4 async-flow + 1 no-sync-calls). Old analysis-step-0..3 tick IDs removed. 49/49 packages pass.

files: internal/command/pr_split_10_pipeline.js (analyzeDiffAsync, createSplitPlanAsync — async versions to use)

sequentialTasks.task: T35: Convert runExecutionStep to async with per-branch yielding
sequentialTasks.description: runExecutionStep() at pr_split_16_tui_core.js calls prSplit.executeSplit(st.planCache) at line 2431 which creates ALL branches sequentially via blocking gitExec() calls. This blocks the event loop for 5-30 seconds total. executeSplitAsync() EXISTS (pr_split_05_execution.js:221) but is NOT called from TUI. Convert to async with per-branch progress: (a) Create startExecutionAsync(s) that launches executeSplitAsync (uses exec.spawn internally) as a Promise. Store in s.executionPending. Start polling via tea.tick(100, 'execution-poll'). (b) Modify executeSplitAsync to emit per-branch progress: after each branch, resolve a progress callback with {branchIdx, branchTotal, branchName, status}. Store in s.executionBranchIdx/Total/Current. (c) Update viewExecutionScreen to show per-branch progress bar during creation. (d) Replace the exec-step-0 tick handler with execution-poll dispatch. (e) Cancel mid-branch via s.executionCancelled flag checked between branches. (f) Also fix exec-step-2 (line ~2475): handleEquivCheckState() internally calls sync verifyEquivalence() — replace with verifyEquivalenceAsync() (pr_split_06_verification.js:596).
acceptanceCriteria: Branch creation shows per-branch progress in real-time (branchIdx/branchTotal/branchName visible)
UI responsive during execution — cancel processes within 1 second
Equivalence check (verifyEquivalenceAsync) is non-blocking
All existing execution tests pass
Zero remaining calls to sync executeSplit/verifyEquivalence from TUI context (verified by grep)
sequentialTasks.deliverables: Replaced sync runExecutionStep with async runExecutionAsync + handleExecutionPoll + startEquivCheck + runEquivCheckAsync + handleEquivPoll. executeSplitAsync used for branch creation with progressFn updating s.executingIdx/executionBranchTotal in real-time. verifyEquivalenceAsync used for equiv check. All 4 exec-step-2 callers updated to use startEquivCheck. Removed dead handleEquivCheckState variable. Added executionRunning/executionError/executionNextStep/executionBranchTotal/executionProgressMsg/equivRunning/equivError to init state. Updated viewExecutionScreen to show branchIdx/branchTotal progress. 13 new tests all PASS.

sequentialTasks.task: T36: Create resolveAsync and convert handleClaudeCheck to non-blocking
sequentialTasks.description: handleClaudeCheck() at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:2049 calls executor.resolve() (pr_split_09_claude.js:32) which runs synchronous exec.execv(['which', 'claude']) and exec.execv(['claude', '--version']), blocking the TUI for 300-500ms+. No async version exists. Steps: (a) In pr_split_09_claude.js, create ClaudeCodeExecutor.prototype.resolveAsync = async function() that uses exec.spawn() + await for all subprocess calls (which, version checks). Return same {command, provider, version, error} shape. (b) In handleClaudeCheck (line 2049), replace synchronous resolve() call with Promise+poll pattern: var p = executor.resolveAsync(); s.claudeCheckPending = p; return tea.tick(50, 'claude-check-poll'). (c) Create handleClaudeCheckPoll that checks resolution. (d) Show animated status during check: 'Resolving binary...' → 'Checking version...' → 'Connected!' or error message. (e) Cache successful result in st.claudeExecutor to avoid re-checking on strategy toggle. (f) On failure, show actionable error: 'Claude not found — install claude CLI or set CLAUDE_COMMAND env var'.
acceptanceCriteria: Test Connection button shows animated status progression ('Resolving binary...' → 'Checking version...' → 'Connected!')
TUI responsive during check — no freeze (resolveAsync uses exec.spawn)
Successful check shows Claude version string
Failed check shows actionable error: 'Claude not found — install claude CLI or set CLAUDE_COMMAND env var'
Cached result in st.claudeExecutor avoids redundant checks on strategy toggle
All Claude-related tests pass

sequentialTasks.task: T37: Convert verifySplit fallback path to async in TUI
sequentialTasks.description: runVerifyBranch() at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:2563 has a fallback path that calls sync prSplit.verifySplit() when CaptureSession is unavailable. verifySplitAsync() EXISTS (pr_split_06_verification.js:410) but is NOT called. Also cleanupBranches() may block. Steps: (a) Replace sync verifySplit(branchName, opts) at line 2563 with Promise: var p = prSplit.verifySplitAsync(branchName, opts); s.verifyPending = p; return tea.tick(100, 'verify-poll'). (b) Create handleVerifyPoll(s) that checks resolution and updates s.verifyResults. (c) Also audit cleanupBranches (pr_split_06_verification.js) — if called from TUI, use cleanupBranchesAsync(). (d) Verify: verification runs without freezing TUI. Progress indicator visible. Cancel works.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification fallback path uses verifySplitAsync and is non-blocking
Progress indicator visible during verification
Cancel works during verification
All existing verification tests pass
Zero remaining sync verifySplit/cleanupBranches calls from TUI context (verified by grep)

sequentialTasks.task: T38: Fix split-view Tab behavior — cycle elements within active pane
sequentialTasks.description: When split-view is enabled, Tab at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:~405 only toggles between wizard/claude panes (s.splitViewFocus). This completely breaks in-pane navigation — user cannot Tab between buttons, cards, or inputs within the wizard pane while split-view is open. Fix: (a) Change Tab behavior when splitViewFocus === 'wizard': Tab cycles through focusable elements within the wizard pane (same as normal mode). Only Ctrl+Tab or a dedicated key (e.g., Ctrl+`) switches between panes. (b) When splitViewFocus === 'claude': Tab still forwards to Claude PTY (standard terminal behavior). Ctrl+Tab switches back to wizard. (c) Update Ctrl+L (toggle split-view) to preserve focus state — don't reset focusIndex when toggling. (d) Update help overlay to show correct keybindings for split-view mode. (e) Add test: Tab in split-view cycles wizard elements, Ctrl+Tab switches panes.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab cycles through wizard focusable elements while in split-view wizard focus
Ctrl+Tab (or designated key) switches between wizard and Claude panes
Claude pane receives Tab as terminal input when Claude pane is focused
Help overlay shows correct keybindings for split-view mode
Focus index preserved when toggling split-view (Ctrl+L)
Existing tab navigation tests pass

sequentialTasks.task: T39: Fix expand/collapse state management — per-item, not global reset
sequentialTasks.description: Collapse handler at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:1314 sets s.expandedVerifyBranch = null, which clears ALL expanded state regardless of which branch was collapsed. The system only supports ONE expanded branch at a time. Also found at lines 2383, 2463. Fix: (a) If the design intent is single-expand (accordion): collapse handler should only clear if the clicked branch matches s.expandedVerifyBranch. Currently it always clears, which is correct for accordion — BUT the expand/collapse zone detection loop (lines 854-859) has a logic issue: the collapse check runs for ALL branches, not just the expanded one, meaning clicking in ANOTHER branch's collapse zone when it's not expanded still fires. Add guard: if (vbranch === s.expandedVerifyBranch) before collapse. (b) Also fix: Advanced Options expand/collapse on CONFIG screen — verify toggle state persists (s.showAdvanced). (c) Also fix: Split detail expansion on PLAN_REVIEW — verify s.expandedSplitIdx toggles correctly and only clears on the correct split. (d) Add explicit visual chevrons: ▶ (collapsed) / ▼ (expanded). (e) Ensure Escape collapses current expanded section before back-nav.
acceptanceCriteria: Collapse handler only fires when the clicked branch matches s.expandedVerifyBranch
Accordion behavior correct — one expanded item at a time
Visual chevrons rendered: ▶ (collapsed) / ▼ (expanded)
Escape collapses current expanded section before triggering back-navigation
All expand/collapse interactions tested (verify, advanced options, split detail)

sequentialTasks.task: T41: Fix inline title editing + navigation conflict in plan editor
sequentialTasks.description: In PLAN_EDITOR, when editing a split title inline (s.editorTitleEditing=true at pr_split_16_tui_core.js:305-341), pressing j/k still triggers handleListNav() (line ~1722) which changes s.selectedFileIdx. This corrupts the edit context: user types 'j' in title → title gets 'j' BUT selection also moves. If user presses Enter, title saves to the wrong split. Fix: (a) In handleListNav() at its definition (~line 1722) or at its call sites (~lines 485, 488), add guard: if (s.editorTitleEditing) return [s, null]; — skip navigation while editing. (b) Similarly guard all navigation keys (j/k/up/down/pgup/pgdn) when any inline edit is active. (c) Add Escape to cancel edit (restore original title). (d) Add Enter to confirm edit (save to correct split index, which is s.editorTitleEditingIdx, NOT s.selectedSplitIdx). (e) Verify: start editing split title → type characters → press j → only 'j' appears in title, no navigation. (f) Verify: start editing → press Escape → original title restored.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation keys (j/k/up/down/pgup/pgdn) disabled during inline title edit (s.editorTitleEditing guard in handleListNav)
Title editing confined to correct split via s.editorTitleEditingIdx (not s.selectedSplitIdx)

Test verifies edit-nav isolation

sequentialTasks.task: T42: Default to Claude strategy when Claude is available
sequentialTasks.description: Currently, prSplit.runtime.mode defaults to 'heuristic'. When Claude is available, it should default to 'auto' (Claude-powered). The user reported heuristic is the default even when Claude is available — this undermines the core value proposition. Fix: (a) In pr_split_16_tui_core.js createWizardModel() (line ~50), set initial strategy based on Claude availability: if prSplitConfig.claude or env CLAUDE_COMMAND → default to 'auto'. (b) On CONFIG screen load, trigger async Claude availability check (the resolveAsync from T36). If Claude found → auto-select 'auto' strategy. If not → auto-select 'heuristic'. (c) Show visual indication: '✓ Claude available — using auto strategy' or '⚠ Claude not found — using heuristic'. (d) User can still manually switch strategy. (e) Persist strategy preference if user manually changes it.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto strategy selected by default when Claude is available (via resolveAsync check)
Heuristic strategy auto-selected as fallback when Claude is unavailable
Visual indication shows selected strategy and Claude status ('✓ Claude available' or '⚠ Claude not found')
Manual strategy override works and persists
No blocking during Claude detection

sequentialTasks.task: T43: Graceful error recovery for stale/missing branch on CONFIG screen
sequentialTasks.description: User reported: on a branch with no commits yet, wizard jumps to Step 7/7 Finalization with error 'cannot determine current branch: fatal: ambiguous argument HEAD'. This is because the config validation at handleConfigState() calls rev-parse HEAD which fails on an empty repo/branch. Fix: (a) On CONFIG screen enter, check for empty repo state: git rev-parse HEAD fails → show friendly message 'No commits on current branch. Please make at least one commit before splitting.' (b) Don't jump to error state — stay on CONFIG, show inline warning. (c) If source branch is set to '(auto-detect)' and auto-detect fails, show dropdown of available branches from `git branch --list`. (d) Validate source branch existence before proceeding: git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<branch>. (e) If target branch doesn't exist, offer to create it or show error. (f) Add graceful handle for detached HEAD state.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty repo (git rev-parse HEAD fails) shows friendly inline warning on CONFIG screen instead of jumping to error state
Auto-detect failure for source branch shows dropdown of available branches from `git branch --list`
Source and target branch existence validated before proceeding (git rev-parse --verify)
Detached HEAD state handled gracefully
All CONFIG validation tests pass

sequentialTasks.task: T44: Mux process output into TUI during long-running operations
sequentialTasks.description: User reported: after clicking Start, the TUI shows 'Processing...' but gives NO feedback about what's happening (e.g., `make` running). There's no way to see or interact with the running process. Fix by muxing process output into a CaptureSession rendered via a tabbed pane in the split-view area: (a) Add a tab bar to the split-view pane area: [Claude] [Output]. Switch via Ctrl+Shift+O or clicking tabs. (b) When analysis starts, create a CaptureSession capturing git command output, display in Output tab. (c) When execution starts (executeSplit), stream per-branch output via CaptureSession in Output tab. (d) When make/tests run during verification, stream make output. (e) Reuse renderClaudePane infrastructure for Output tab (ANSI rendering, scrolling, viewport). (f) Minimum terminal height guard: if <15 lines, show output tab but hide Claude tab. (g) tea.tick-based polling for output updates (100ms). (h) Register zone.mark() IDs for tab bar clicks and output scroll-wheel. (i) Allow scrolling through output history.
acceptanceCriteria: All long-running operations (analysis, execution, verification) show live output in TUI via Output tab
Git commands, make output, and branch creation output all visible in Output tab
Output panel is scrollable with scroll history
ANSI rendering intact in Output tab (reuses renderClaudePane infrastructure)
Tab bar with [Claude] [Output] tabs switchable via Ctrl+Shift+O or click
No blocking — output polling via tea.tick at 100ms

sequentialTasks.task: T45: Auto-attach Claude pane when Claude spawns during wizard
sequentialTasks.description: Currently the user must manually open the Claude split-view (Ctrl+L). User reported 'Waiting for Claude output...' with no visible output pane. When Claude spawns in the pipeline, the TUI should auto-open the split-view. Steps: (a) In the MCP pipeline (automatedSplit), after Claude executor spawns and is attached to tuiMux (T33), set a flag or emit an event: s.claudeJustSpawned = true. (b) In handleAutoSplitPoll, detect s.claudeJustSpawned and auto-enable split-view: s.splitViewEnabled = true; s.splitViewFocus = 'wizard'. Only if terminal height >= 12 lines. (c) Show transient notification: 'Claude connected — Ctrl+L to toggle, Ctrl+] for passthrough'. (d) If user manually closes split-view (s.claudeManuallyDismissed), don't re-open. (e) Show persistent Claude badge in status bar when Claude is running (clickable to re-open split-view). (f) On Claude crash/exit, auto-close split-view and show notification.
acceptanceCriteria: Split-view auto-opens when Claude spawns (s.splitViewEnabled set to true)
Claude output visible immediately after spawn via childScreen()
Manual dismiss (Ctrl+L) respected — auto-open does not re-trigger after s.claudeManuallyDismissed
Status bar shows persistent clickable Claude badge when Claude is running
Small terminals (<12 lines) skip auto-open
Split-view auto-closes on Claude crash/exit with notification

sequentialTasks.task: T46: Claude inline feedback during PLAN_GENERATION — detect questions, send answers
sequentialTasks.description: During PLAN_GENERATION with auto strategy, Claude may ask clarification questions (visible in terminal output). Enhance: (a) In handleAutoSplitPoll, detect when Claude's screen (s.claudeScreen) contains question markers (?, '>', 'please', 'clarif', 'would you') combined with idle state (no output for >2s via tuiMux.lastActivityMs()). (b) When detected, show inline prompt at bottom of analysis screen: 'Claude asks: [last line]. Type response:'. (c) User types response, Enter sends via tuiMux.writeToChild(response + '\r'). (d) Claude pane auto-scrolls. (e) Track conversation in s.claudeConversations[] for history. (f) Keyboard shortcut (Enter or Ctrl+R when question detected) to focus input. (g) Register zone.mark() IDs for the inline prompt input field and submit button. (h) Create 20+ representative Claude output snapshots as test fixtures for question-detection validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude questions detected via question markers (?, '>', 'please', 'clarif', 'would you') combined with idle state (no output for >2s)
False-positive rate <10% on 20+ representative Claude output test fixtures
User can respond directly from wizard via inline prompt with Enter to send
Response sent to Claude via tuiMux.writeToChild(response + '\r') and pane auto-scrolls
Conversation history tracked in s.claudeConversations[]
Detection non-blocking — only shown when confidence is high (idle >2s + question markers + no recent tool calls)
zone.mark() IDs registered for inline prompt input field and submit button

sequentialTasks.task: T47: Ask Claude on PLAN_REVIEW with inline response rendering
sequentialTasks.description: PLAN_REVIEW has Ask Claude button (T16). Enhance: (a) When clicked, open inline text input below plan cards. (c) Parse Claude MCP response (waitForLogged with 'planFeedback' tool) and display inline. (d) If Claude suggests modifications, offer 'Apply Suggestions' button. (f) Track Q&A history in s.planQA[]. (g) Keep conversation context (multiple rounds). (h) Register zone.mark() IDs for text input, submit button, and 'Apply Suggestions' button.
acceptanceCriteria: Ask Claude button opens inline text input pre-filled with context ('Regarding split [selected split name]: ')

Apply Suggestions button modifies planCache with visible diff of what changed

sequentialTasks.task: T48: Claude context injection in ERROR_RESOLUTION with diagnostic info
sequentialTasks.description: When entering ERROR_RESOLUTION, Claude should receive diagnostic context automatically. Steps: (a) On transition to ERROR_RESOLUTION, if Claude is alive: compile error context (error message, stack trace, affected branch, recent git log, build output snapshot). (b) Send to Claude via sendToHandle as structured prompt. (c) Wait for Claude diagnosis via waitForLogged ('errorDiagnosis' tool). (d) Display Claude's diagnosis inline on ERROR_RESOLUTION screen. (e) If Claude suggests fix commands, offer 'Apply Fix' button. (f) Apply Fix: run commands via exec.spawn (non-blocking), show CaptureSession output, re-verify. (g) Fallback to manual if Claude unavailable. (h) Register zone.mark() IDs for 'Apply Fix' button and diagnosis display area.
acceptanceCriteria: Error context (error message, stack trace, affected branch, recent git log, build output) auto-sent to Claude on ERROR_RESOLUTION entry
Claude's diagnosis displayed inline on ERROR_RESOLUTION screen

sequentialTasks.task: T57: Session persistence — wizard state checkpoint and resume after crash/cancel
sequentialTasks.description: Checkpoint system: (a) Save state on every wizard state transition (JSON to ~/.config/osm/pr-split-session.json). (b) On startup, check for session file → offer 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh'. (c) Persist the COMPLETED RESULTS of async operations and the current step index — do NOT serialize in-flight Promises (they serialize to {}). On resume, skip completed steps and re-launch any incomplete async operation from the last checkpoint step-progress state. State includes: wizard state, plan cache, analysis cache, groups cache, execution step index and per-branch results, error context, Claude session info. (d) Delete on successful completion. (e) Session lock file (check PID). (f) Handle stale locks.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel→restart offers 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh' options
Crash→restart offers 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh' options
Successful completion deletes session file at ~/.config/osm/pr-split-session.json
'Start Fresh' clears all persisted state
Session lock file prevents concurrent instances (checks PID)
Stale lock files (dead PID) handled gracefully
Step-progress state (not in-flight Promises) properly serialized; resume re-enters at correct wizard step

sequentialTasks.task: T70: Claude interaction in EQUIV_CHECK — tree-hash mismatch diagnosis
sequentialTasks.description: EQUIV_CHECK phase has no Claude interaction. When equivalence check shows a tree-hash mismatch, Claude should diagnose the issue before routing to ERROR_RESOLUTION. Add: (a) If equiv check fails (tree hashes don't match), and Claude is alive: send diagnostic context to Claude with diff of expected vs actual trees. (b) Claude analyzes mismatch and suggests root cause (e.g., 'File X was in the source branch but not in any split branch'). (c) Display diagnosis inline on EQUIV_CHECK screen. (d) Offer 'Fix Automatically' if Claude suggests a specific fix (e.g., add missing file to a split). (e) Register zone.mark() for diagnosis area and Fix button. (f) Fallback to ERROR_RESOLUTION if Claude unavailable or no fix suggested.
acceptanceCriteria: Equivalence mismatch sends diff of expected vs actual trees to Claude for diagnosis
Claude's root cause analysis displayed inline on EQUIV_CHECK screen
Fix Automatically button available when Claude suggests a specific fix
Fallback to ERROR_RESOLUTION when Claude is unavailable or no fix suggested
zone.mark() IDs registered for diagnosis area and Fix button
All operations non-blocking

sequentialTasks.task: T71: Claude interaction in FINALIZATION — draft PR descriptions
sequentialTasks.description: FINALIZATION phase shows a summary report but has no Claude interaction. Add: (a) After finalization report renders, if Claude is alive, automatically send split plan summary to Claude and request PR description drafts for each split branch. (b) Display drafts inline below each split summary: 'Suggested PR Title: [title]\nSuggested PR Body: [body]'. (c) 'Copy to Clipboard' button per draft. (d) Option to edit drafts inline before copying. (e) Register zone.mark() for copy buttons and edit areas. (f) Works asynchronously — report shows immediately, drafts populate as Claude responds.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude drafts PR title and body for each split branch after finalization report renders
Drafts rendered inline below each split summary
Copy-to-clipboard button works per draft
Drafts editable inline before copying
zone.mark() IDs registered for copy buttons and edit areas

sequentialTasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md per Keep a Changelog 1.1.0: (a) 'Added' entries for all new features (ANSI Claude pane, input forwarding, auto-attach, search, session persistence, Claude interaction in all wizard phases, etc.). (b) 'Changed' entries for async conversions, design doc rewrite. (c) 'Fixed' entries for event loop blocking, tab navigation, expand/collapse, stale branch error. (d) Proofread.

All changes from T32-T71 documented under appropriate categories (Added, Changed, Fixed)

continuousVerification.description: Execute `make` which runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. ALL must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu, windows, macos). Use `make test` for fast feedback, `make` for full verification before commit.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Verify coverage for new code using `make cover`. Target >=90% for TUI functions, 100% for state machine transitions.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ALL checks must pass. NEVER commit with failing checks.
Rule of Two MANDATORY before every commit. Two contiguous issue-free reviews of the exact same diff.
JS runs in Goja event loop. Never call exec.execv() in update() or Promise continuations. Use exec.spawn() + await. Use tea.tick() to yield.
Claude pane MUST use ANSI rendering via Mux.ChildScreen()/vtm.RenderFullScreen(). screenshot() is for diagnostics/assertions only.
Interactive Claude keyboard input forwarding via Mux.WriteToChild() when Claude pane is focused. Reserve Ctrl+L/Tab/Ctrl+C/Ctrl+]/F1/? for wizard control.

tuiMux bootstrap MUST be complete: Go creates Mux → injects as JS global → pipeline spawns Claude → attaches to Mux → poll/render cycle reads childScreen(). Any break in this chain = dead Claude pane.
